description: A/B comparison of x-probe: old (no evidence grading) vs new (4-tier Evidence Quality Labels). Same test idea, same user answers.

method.idea: 실시간 협업 문서 편집기 — Google Docs 같은 CRDT 기반 동시 편집

user_answers.premise_1_market_demand: 우리 팀 내부에서 필요하다고 느낌 (no external data)
user_answers.premise_2_crdt_feasibility: Yjs 라이브러리가 있다 (specific tool, unproven integration)
user_answers.premise_3_team_capability: 비슷한 걸 해본 적은 없지만 할 수 있을 것 같다 (belief)

method.condition_a: Old — no evidence grading, confidence + fragility only
method.condition_b: New — 4-tier evidence grades (assumption < heuristic < data-backed < validated), grade-calibrated questioning, fatal+assumption blocks PROCEED

old.evidence_visibility: none — all premises look equally grounded

new.ordering: fragility first, then evidence grade within tier (assumption first)

old.question_strategy: uniform — same pattern for all premises
old.example: What evidence do you have? → vague/specific/none follow-up

new.example_assumption: This is an untested belief. Cheapest way to test?
new.example_heuristic: When did you last see this? What was different?
new.example_data_backed: What would make this data misleading?
new.example_validated: When was this validated? What changed since?
new.grade_transitions: grades can go up or down after user answers

old.reasoning: Some premises weak but not refuted, qualitative warning
old.explanation: Old system has no hard blocker for belief-based fatal premises. A persuasive Socratic answer could pass.

new.reasoning: fatal+assumption hard blocker — P1(demand) and P3(capability) both FATAL+assumption
new.explanation: New rule: fatal premise graded assumption → PROCEED forbidden. No amount of persuasion bypasses this.

evidence_visibility.improvement: 0→100% — every premise now has explicit evidence classification

verdict_safety.description: Can a belief-only fatal premise pass to PROCEED?

grade_tracking.improvement: Evidence grades tracked across phases with up/down transitions

xbuild_evidence_gaps.improvement: +1 section — Evidence Gaps with specific validation actions

verdict_same_for_this_case.note: Same verdict here, but the critical difference: old system COULD have been PROCEED with a persuasive answer. New system CANNOT — structural blocker.

conclusions: Both systems produce RETHINK for this test case — but for fundamentally different reasons
Old system: qualitative warning ('some premises weak') — a persuasive Socratic answer could flip to PROCEED
New system: structural blocker (fatal+assumption) — no amount of persuasion bypasses the evidence requirement
Evidence Summary (🟢0 / 🟡1 / 🔴4) makes the idea's epistemic foundation immediately visible
Grade-calibrated questioning spends time proportionally: heavy on assumptions, light on validated premises
x-build receives actionable Evidence Gaps section — can auto-schedule validation tasks early in plan
